name: Elizabeth Holmes
lesson: "Faking it until you make it" is a vibe. Faking blood test results is a felony. Know the difference before you pitch your Series A.

links.label: The Inventor — HBO documentary on how the lie scaled
Bad Blood — Carreyrou's inside account of the cover-up

name: Adam Neumann
lesson: You can convince investors you're a real estate company, a tech company, and a consciousness movement simultaneously — right up until you file the S-1.

links.label: WeWork: Or the Making and Breaking of a $47B Unicorn
WSJ — The IPO filing that ended everything

name: Sam Bankman-Fried
lesson: Effective altruism does not work retroactively. You cannot donate your way out of an 8-billion-dollar shortfall with customer funds.

links.label: Going Infinite — Michael Lewis inside account

name: Brian Chesky
lesson: When COVID killed 80% of revenue overnight, Chesky laid off 25% of staff in one of the most transparent CEO moves on record. Then IPO'd at $100B. Honesty scales.

links.label: Chesky's 2020 layoff letter — the one everyone studies
Tim Ferriss — Chesky on redesigning Airbnb post-COVID

name: Stewart Butterfield
lesson: Failed game studio became a $27B company. The lesson isn't "pivot." It's that Butterfield had the self-awareness to recognize the real product hiding inside his failure.

links.label: How I Built This — Butterfield on two failed games and Slack

name: Reed Hastings
lesson: Qwikster was a masterclass in how to lose 800,000 subscribers in a quarter. Hastings reversed it, owned the failure publicly, and rebuilt trust. The apology was the product.

links.label: Netflix vs. Blockbuster — the pivot that almost killed them
No Rules Rules — Hastings on what he got wrong
